refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the operational context ensures clarity and testability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability status to relevant stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use real-time link health monitoring and predictive analytics.
DP3: Integrate link status reporting via telemetry interface.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Interaction with Teleoperation and Safety Services for failover coordination
coupling_check.notes: Redundancy and failover logic are localized to network management, minimizing cross-module coupling.

assumptions_risks: Risk of failover delay impacting link availability.
High safety criticality requires robust failover mechanisms.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding predictive analytics (DP2) in future refinement to enhance proactive failure management.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded systems responsible for network link management and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for physical and logical network connections supporting redundancy and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Reports link status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as network hardware and failover logic reside here; low diffusion due to limited external dependencies.
Receives link status but does not control failover; moderate diffusion due to dependency on Vehicle.
Monitors safety alerts related to link failures but does not manage link availability directly.
Coordinates overall system but does not implement failover logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Network link availability management is best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and ensure rapid failover.
containment_risks: Potential delays in communication with Teleoperation module during failover.
Vehicle module complexity may increase with added network management responsibilities.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate interface performance and consider modularizing failover logic if Vehicle module complexity grows.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and meets availability targets.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion as failover logic is localized in Vehicle module.
External interfaces are well-defined and limited.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within Vehicle module aligns with physical network hardware ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Link availability can be verified via telemetry data and network diagnostics within Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for link availability to Vehicle module.

recommended_revisions: Monitor Vehicle module complexity growth due to network management responsibilities.
Plan for integration testing with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns a suitable IM with clear interfaces and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Report link availability status to relevant stakeholders.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Telemetry interface can reliably report link status.
